draft.introduction: Jerome’s preface to the Vulgate Bible defends his decision to translate directly from the Hebrew rather than relying on the Septuagint. This methodological choice shaped the Western biblical tradition and established the principle of Hebraica veritas.

translation.text: I have received letters demanding that I set the Septuagint translation in its proper context and bring the Latin books closer to the Hebrew original. Those who wish to know the old translation have it; our new work is offered not to replace the old but for those who wish to learn the Hebrew truth.
translation.original_text: Hic prologus Scripturarum quasi galeatum principium omnibus libris quos de hebraeo vertimus in latinum convenire potest

translator_notes.note: Jerome’s insistence on Hebraica veritas (Hebrew truth) over the Septuagint put him at odds with Augustine, who feared that diverging from the Greek text used by the churches would cause scandal. This debate over source texts for translation anticipated the humanist-Reformation conflicts by a millennium.
